results.description: Reweighing adjusts instance influence across protected groups before model fitting to reduce structural imbalance in outcomes. Fairness improved under DPD; accuracy trade-off could not be estimated from available signals.

results.scenario_reason: Dataset domain scenario 'education' detected (confidence=0.67) using dataset description/columns; reweighing is prioritized by domain mitigation policy.
results.description: Disparate Impact Remover repairs group-conditioned outcome distortions to reduce indirect bias patterns. Fairness improved under DPD; accuracy trade-off could not be estimated from available signals.

Finds the decision threshold per group minimising |rate−60.0%| (global median) + 0.5×(1−accuracy) on model-predicted probabilities. Fairness improved under DPD; accuracy trade-off could not be estimated from available signals.

Reject Option Classification shifts uncertain boundary decisions to favor disadvantaged groups while preserving utility. Fairness did not improve under DPD; accuracy trade-off could not be estimated from available signals. ⚠ Invalid: method did not reduce bias.

mitigation.trade_off_summary: Bias changed from 83.5 to 67.1 (↓16.4 pts, 19.6% magnitude). DPD changed 0.7000 → 0.2800; DIR tracked from 0.2632 to 0.6216.  Estimated accuracy after mitigation: 61.8%. Fairness improved under selected metrics. No method guarantees perfect fairness.

audit_result.summary:
The system exhibits critical bias with a score of 83.5, indicating severe fairness concerns. This audit highlights significant disparities in outcomes based on Gender.

Specifically, Males have a 95.0% pass rate, dramatically higher than Females at 25.0%. This 70.0% difference is a major concern.

Key metrics like Demographic Parity Difference (0.7000) and Disparate Impact Ratio (0.2632) confirm substantial outcome inequality, requiring immediate attention.
key_findings: The overall bias score is 83.5, categorized as Critical.
Male pass rate is 95.0%, significantly higher than Female pass rate of 25.0%.
Demographic Parity Difference is 0.7000, indicating a 70.0% disparity.
Disparate Impact Ratio is 0.2632, falling below the 0.80 threshold.
Theil inequality index of 0.1814 shows significant outcome inequality.
recommendations: Investigate root causes for the 25.0% Female pass rate.
Review and revise criteria to reduce the 0.7000 DPD.
Implement targeted interventions to improve Female outcomes.
Continuously monitor fairness metrics for sustained improvement.

plain_language.overall: This audit reveals critical fairness issues, with a bias score of 83.5. Males pass at a rate of 95.0%, significantly higher than Females at 25.0%, indicating substantial outcome disparities.
plain_language.demographic_parity_difference: There is a 70.0% difference in the pass rates between the Male and Female groups.
plain_language.disparate_impact_ratio: The pass rate for Females is only 26.32% of the pass rate for Males.
plain_language.statistical_test: The observed differences in outcomes between groups are statistically significant.
plain_language.theil_index: The Theil index is 0.1814, reflecting considerable inequality in outcomes across the sensitive groups.
plain_language.performance_gap: The Performance Gap is 0.00, indicating no difference in overall performance between groups.
